title: Voters are angry with Washington, and other takeaways from the Colorado primaries
description: A sitting Democratic House member and a sitting senator both lost key races in the state.

title: Trump to honor a president he sees as a peer — Theodore Roosevelt
description: The president plans to fly to rural North Dakota to open Roosevelt’s library as aides tout comparisons between the two.

title: Supreme Court deals some blows to Trump's agenda but leaves him with more expansive powers
description: President Trump has trumpeted his victories and sought workarounds for his losses.

title: A federal law bans late voter roll purges. Republicans are pushing to reinterpret it
description: There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.

title: The majority of Americans are proud but worry about direction of the country
description: As the U.S. approaches its 250th birthday, Americans reflect on the state of the nation in the latest NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.

title: In Colorado primaries, a democratic socialist wins; Bennet's governor bid ends
description: Democrats nominated their candidate to try to unseat a Republican House member and tapped the state's attorney general as a frontrunner for governor.

title: How America has celebrated milestone birthdays, from world fairs to the World Cup
description: Two founding fathers died on America's 50th birthday, which was marked with toasts and parades. Over time, anniversary celebrations became more extravagant — and more controversial.

title: Months after he sent a harsh email to ICE, agents tracked him to his home and a hotel
description: Federal agents went to a Rochester man's home to warn him that an email he sent five months ago could be an illegal threat. He was on vacation but another agent found his hotel hundreds of miles away.

title: Trump’s income topped $2 billion in 2025, boosted by crypto, coin ventures
description: Almost 1,000 pages of financial disclosure forms show that the president’s earnings far outpaced his $600 million total income in 2024.

title: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros poised to become the first Gen Z woman in Congress
description: Kiros, a political newcomer, topped 15-term-incumbent Diana DeGette in the Democratic primary in Colorado's 1st Congressional District, a reliably blue House seat.

title: Trump calls on Congress to end birthright citizenship after court defeat
description: The Supreme Court's ruling in the birthright citizenship case poses a political challenge for President Donald Trump.

title: Supreme Court sides with GOP, loosens campaign spending rules
description: The decision is the latest in which the Supreme Court has weighed in on campaign finance rules.

title: Supreme Court upholds principle that almost all born on U.S. soil are American
description: The ruling striking down President Donald Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship reaffirmed that the 14th Amendment automatically confers citizenship on any child born in the U.S.

title: Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender women in female athletics
description: The ruling is the latest blow to the LGBTQ+ community, which has faced repeated losses at the Supreme Court in recent years.

title: Rep. Tom Kean returns to Congress, says depression is why he went missing for months
description: The New Jersey Republican was missing for months with no explanation for his constituents. He explained in a House floor speech that after his diagnosis, there was no timeline for recovery.

title: Supreme Court strikes down limits on political party spending
description: At issue in the case was a post-Watergate law that Congress passed to limit the amount of money individuals can give to political parties.

title: Rep. Gregory Meeks, D-N.Y., on the Trump administration's Iran briefing
description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Rep. Gregory Meeks of New York, the top Democrat on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, about the Trump administration's Iran briefing to lawmakers.

title: Supreme Court expands Trump’s power over the federal bureaucracy
description: The justices allowed the president to fire a Democratic member of the Federal Trade Commission, striking down a nearly century-old precedent intended to insulate independent agencies from political influence by the executive.

title: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
description: The decision is a setback for Trump, who alleged that Cook committed mortgage fraud. She is the first Black woman to serve on the Fed board.

title: Housing bill that Trump refuses to sign heads to his desk
description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is set to send a bipartisan housing bill to President Donald Trump’s desk Monday, but it is unclear whether he will sign it into law.

title: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in Carroll case
description: A 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting journalist E. Jean Carroll. The decision Monday leaves in place a ruling affirming the judgment.
